title: Aloo Gobi

seo.title: Authentic Pakistani Aloo Gobi Recipe (Vegetarian)
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Pakistani Aloo Gobi, a flavorful vegetarian curry with potatoes and cauliflower. Easy step-by-step recipe for a delicious meal.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Pakistani Aloo Gobi curry served in a bowl with fresh cilantro garnish.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this authentic Pakistani Aloo Gobi, a hearty and flavorful vegetarian dish perfect for any meal.

description: Aloo Gobi is a beloved Pakistani vegetarian dish, combining tender potatoes and crisp cauliflower in a rich, aromatic tomato-based curry. Flavored with traditional spices, it offers a comforting and wholesome experience that is both simple to prepare and incredibly satisfying. This hearty staple is perfect with naan or rice, bringing the authentic taste of Pakistan to your table.

ingredients.notes: slit lengthwise or chopped, adjust to taste

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables
instructions.text: Wash and cut potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Wash and cut cauliflower into medium-sized florets. Keep them separate as they have different cooking times. Prepare your chopped onion, pureed tomatoes, ginger-garlic paste, and slit green chilies.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat cooking oil in a large pot or karahi over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomatoes and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the pureed tomatoes and cook until the oil starts to separate from the masala, about 5 minutes. Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes, adding a splash of water if the masala sticks to the bottom.

instructions.title: Cook Potatoes
instructions.text: Add the cubed potatoes to the masala. Mix thoroughly to coat the potatoes with the spices. Add about 1/4 cup of water, cover the pot, and let the potatoes cook on medium-low heat for 10-12 minutes, or until they are halfway tender.

instructions.title: Incorporate Cauliflower and Simmer
instructions.text: Once the potatoes are partially cooked, add the cauliflower florets to the pot. Gently mix them with the potatoes and masala. Cover the pot again and continue to cook on low heat for another 10-15 minutes, or until both the potatoes and cauliflower are tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the vegetables are cooked through and tender, add the slit green chilies and garam masala powder. Give it a final gentle stir. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot.

tips: To achieve perfectly cooked vegetables, ensure your potatoes and cauliflower are cut into similar-sized pieces for even cooking. You can lightly fry the cauliflower separately before adding it to the curry for a slightly crispier texture and to prevent it from becoming mushy.

For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a pinch of fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) along with the garam masala at the end of the cooking process. It adds a wonderful aromatic depth to the Aloo Gobi.

Aloo Gobi tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld together. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave.
article: Aloo Gobi is a beloved Pakistani Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
